summary: Release-machinery / test-infrastructure / docs / hygiene chunk. No new Alma API surface; 0 SANDBOX tests recommended. All 33 ACs are exercised by meta-tests, unit tests, docstring/doc edits, or repo-hygiene assertions — none require live SANDBOX behaviour.

unmappable.ac: The three new meta-tests exist under `tests/meta/` and pass on `main`.
unmappable.reason: Meta-test addition (tests/meta/test_no_hardcoded_version.py, test_top_level_docs_match_all.py, test_docs_version_matches_pyproject.py). No Alma API surface — verified by `poetry run pytest tests/meta/`, not by a SANDBOX call.

unmappable.ac: Each meta-test FAILS on a synthetic regression: re-add a hardcoded `__version__`, remove a `__all__` symbol from the README table, or downgrade a `Version:` heading to a stale value.
unmappable.reason: Meta-test mutation testing. Verified by locally injecting the regression and re-running pytest; no SANDBOX path.

unmappable.ac: `docs/RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md` Phase F pytest line includes `tests/meta/`.
unmappable.reason: Documentation-only change. Verified by reading the file, not by an API call.

unmappable.ac: The release plan template includes the wheel-version-matches-pyproject shell check between build and upload.
unmappable.reason: Release-plan documentation edit. No runtime / API behaviour.

unmappable.ac: The TestPyPI smoke step in the release plan reads `almaapitk.__version__` dynamically; no hardcoded version string in the success message.
unmappable.reason: Release-plan documentation edit. Exercised at release time on TestPyPI, not against Alma SANDBOX.

unmappable.ac: `poetry run pytest tests/meta/ -q` exits 0 and runs in < 5 seconds.
unmappable.reason: Local pytest invocation. No Alma SANDBOX endpoint involved.

unmappable.ac: `Users(client_stub).logger.name` matches what `alma_logging.get_logger('users', ...)` returns.
unmappable.reason: Internal refactor; verified by the new unit test `tests/unit/domains/test_users_init.py` using a stub client. No SANDBOX path — the assertion is on logger configuration, not HTTP behaviour.

unmappable.ac: Instantiating `Users` does NOT create `sb_log_file.log` or `prod_log_file.log` in the test's working directory.
unmappable.reason: Filesystem side-effect assertion. Verified by unit test using tmp_path / cwd inspection; not observable via any Alma endpoint.

unmappable.ac: No INFO-or-higher records appear on `sys.stdout` when `Users` is instantiated.
unmappable.reason: Stdout-capture assertion (caplog / capsys). Unit-level concern; no SANDBOX call required.

unmappable.ac: `grep -n 'sys.path.append' src/almaapitk/domains/users.py` returns nothing.
unmappable.reason: Source-tree grep assertion. Not testable against an API.

unmappable.ac: Existing tests (`pytest tests/unit/domains/test_users.py`) still pass unchanged.
unmappable.reason: Existing-unit-test regression check. No new SANDBOX coverage needed.

unmappable.ac: The narrowed `except` in `acquisition.py:1814` still passes existing `receive_item` tests.
unmappable.reason: Internal refactor in the JSON-fallback path of an existing method. The receive_item happy path is already covered by existing unit tests (mocked); the narrowed except clause only changes which exception types are caught — not observable via a fresh SANDBOX read. SANDBOX coverage of `receive_item` itself was established in the acquisitions chunk.

unmappable.ac: The new ast guard test passes on the current tree (after the fix).
unmappable.reason: Meta-test (AST walker over src/almaapitk/). Verified by pytest, not by a SANDBOX call.

unmappable.ac: The new ast guard test FAILS if a bare `except:` is reintroduced anywhere under `src/almaapitk/`.
unmappable.reason: Mutation-style assertion on a meta-test. Verified by locally re-introducing a bare except and re-running pytest.

unmappable.ac: The existing `tests/meta/test_logging_policy.py` pattern is followed (same imports, same parametrize style).
unmappable.reason: Test-file structural convention. Verified by code review, not by an API call.

unmappable.ac: New test named `test_update_letter_sends_xml_body_regression_114` (or in a per-issue file under `tests/unit/regressions/`).
unmappable.reason: Pure test-only addition (mocked unit test). The Configuration.update_letter behaviour was already shipped in v0.4.x and exercised against SANDBOX during the original chunk; this is R10 backfill, not a new endpoint.

unmappable.ac: Docstring quotes the Alma `60105 "JSON is not supported for this API."` error and references issue #114.
unmappable.reason: Docstring content of a unit test. Not observable via a SANDBOX call.

unmappable.ac: Assertions: the captured request's content-type header equals `application/xml`; the body is a `str` (not a `dict` or `json.dumps`-encoded string with a JSON content-type).
unmappable.reason: Unit-test assertion on a mocked HTTP request. Verifies the request-construction layer; not a fresh SANDBOX behaviour the chunk introduced.

unmappable.ac: The test FAILS on `git revert 2d20ab3` and PASSES on the current `main`.
unmappable.reason: R10 reproducer test, verified locally by reverting the fix commit. No SANDBOX path.

unmappable.ac: `tests/unit/regressions/` exists as a directory with an `__init__.py`.
unmappable.reason: Source-tree structure assertion. Verified by `ls` / `find`, not by an API call.

unmappable.ac: `CLAUDE.md` R10 section names the canonical home.
unmappable.reason: Documentation-only change in a project file. Not testable against an API.

unmappable.ac: The cumulative regression suite is `pytest tests/unit/regressions/` (no further intersection logic).
unmappable.reason: Process / convention declaration. Verified by inspection of the test invocation; no SANDBOX behaviour.

unmappable.ac: The t-41-3 comment exists at the top of the file.
unmappable.reason: Comment-only edit in an existing sandbox-test file. Not observable via API.

unmappable.ac: The docstring has a "Known caveat" section that names Alma error `401129` and explains the cache lag.
unmappable.reason: Docstring-only change on Users.create_user_request. No behaviour change — no SANDBOX assertion to make.

unmappable.ac: The docstring recommends a caller-side retry with a brief sleep (link to t-41-3 as a worked example).
unmappable.reason: Docstring content. Not observable via API.

unmappable.ac: Existing tests still pass (this is doc-only).
unmappable.reason: Regression baseline. No new SANDBOX coverage required because no behaviour changed.

unmappable.ac: `git status` after the cleanup commit shows zero `chunks/*/_swagger_errors_*.json` files tracked.
unmappable.reason: Repo-hygiene / .gitignore assertion. Verified via git, not via API.

unmappable.ac: `find chunks -name '_swagger_errors_*.json'` still lists them on disk (regeneratable artifacts).
unmappable.reason: Filesystem assertion. Not testable against an API.

unmappable.ac: A second invocation of any chunk's fetch-swagger-codes step regenerates the file without surfacing it in `git status`.
unmappable.reason: Chunk-pipeline / .gitignore behaviour. Verified by re-running fetch-swagger-codes locally; not an Alma SANDBOX call.

unmappable.ac: A unit test deterministically asserts the close-then-switch outcome.
unmappable.reason: Client-internal contract (session lifecycle). Tested with a mocked unit test in `tests/unit/client/test_context_manager.py`; the close-then-switch path never reaches Alma — by design it is asserted to raise before any HTTP call.

unmappable.ac: If the chosen outcome is "re-init session", a follow-up `client.get(...)` succeeds in the test (with a mocked session).
unmappable.reason: Conditional on a path not chosen here (implementation raises a clear error instead). Either branch is unit-level — the spec explicitly says 'with a mocked session'.

unmappable.ac: If the chosen outcome is "raise clear error", the docstring of `switch_environment` documents it.
unmappable.reason: Docstring assertion on the chosen implementation. Not observable via API.

notes: This chunk is release-quality / test-infrastructure / docs / hygiene work driven by the 2026-05-12 release-0.4.x review. By design it adds no new Alma API endpoint coverage — its value is preventing another 0.4.x-style yank cycle, not exercising new SANDBOX behaviour.

Diff scope confirms zero new endpoints: src/ changes are an AlmaAPIClient.switch_environment guard (#138, mocked), a narrowed except clause in Acquisitions.receive_item's XML fallback (#133, internal refactor — happy path already covered), and Users.__init__ logger refactor + create_user_request docstring (#132, #136 — no new endpoints).

All eight issues are covered by the unit / meta tests they themselves ship (tests/meta/, tests/unit/regressions/, tests/unit/domains/test_users_init.py, tests/unit/client/test_context_manager.py, tests/unit/regressions/test_issue_114.py). The R10 regression suite for the 0.4.x cycle is the deliverable.
Recommendation to operator: run `poetry run pytest tests/meta/ tests/unit/ -q` and `poetry run python scripts/smoke_import.py`; no SANDBOX session needed for this chunk.
